meta.strategy: Stack A — Solar Trough Dual-Charge (HE09-11) + AS Hold (HE12-18) + Evening AS Stack (HE19) + Dual Peak DA Discharge (HE20-21) + Post-Discharge NonSpin (HE22-24)
meta.starting_soc_assumption: Near-zero assumed (post-discharge depletion pattern from prior days). Confirm via Tenaska HSL/PMin before 10:00 CT. See Risk 3 contingency in report for SoC != 0 handling.
meta.as_playbook_prior: MISSING — recommend_as_position.py not present. AS allocation based on Smartbidder DA Ancillary Forecasted prices + structural patterns from prior-day learnings.
key_design_decisions: Charge HE09-11 at DA $19.07-$20.62: Solar trough cheapest window. Net load trough 23,827 MW at HE10. DA charge preferred over RT due to oversupply risk and positive DA-RT spread.

HE19 AS-heavy: NonSpin DA $20.67 + RRS DA $12.10 + ECRS DA $3.75 — structural pre-peak AS stack. All 80 MW capacity committed to AS before main discharge. Structural ECRS/NonSpin spike at HE19 confirmed from 2026-05-24/25/26 learnings.
HE20 DA discharge: Smartbidder $60.01 x 0.80 = $48.01 calibrated. P(DA>RT)=74.8%. DA-RT spread +$19.82. Strongest short-DA signal of the day.
HE21 DA discharge: Smartbidder $53.42 x 0.80 = $42.74 calibrated. P(DA>RT)=72.9%. DA-RT spread +$14.73. DA discharge confirmed.
HE22 NonSpin 50 MW at $27.39 DA: Highest NonSpin opportunity post-discharge. SoC=0 compatible. Dominant AS revenue driver for this schedule day.

RegUp NOT offered any hour: Full capacity committed to energy discharge HE20-21. RegUp at HE20 ($34.00) is attractive but $48.01 energy price wins. RegUp requires headroom not available during full discharge.
ECRS HE07-08 structural window: 40 MW DA each hour. Confirmed structural pattern from prior weeks.
20% Smartbidder peak price correction per 2026-05-24 learnings.

revenue_summary.calibration_note: HE20 DA Smartbidder $60.01 x0.80=$48.01; HE21 DA Smartbidder $53.42 x0.80=$42.74. RT AS drag ~20% per 2026-05-25 benchmark ratio. HE22 NonSpin $27.39 DA is the key swing item — range_high assumes it clears near forecast; range_low assumes $5/MWh clearing.
revenue_summary.key_revenue_driver: HE22 NonSpin DA $27.39 x 50 MW = $1,370 gross. This single AS slot contributes ~17% of total expected revenue. Monitor DA clearing carefully.

HE01.note: SoC=0. NonSpin 30 MW DA at $6.28/MWh = $188 gross. RT drag est ~-$38 (20%) net ~$150. P(DA<RT)=55% — night RT slightly firm but not enough to justify DA charge at $27.97.

HE02.note: SoC=0. NonSpin 30 MW DA at $4.52/MWh = $135 gross. RT drag est ~-$27 net ~$108. P(DA<RT)=60.6% — overnight RT premium signal, but not charging here (cheapest hours are HE09-11).

HE03.note: SoC=0. NonSpin 20 MW (reduced MW vs HE01-02 — low AS price at $3.07). Not charging here: solar trough at HE09-10 provides $4/MWh price advantage over HE03 ($23.31 vs $19.07). P(DA<RT)=62% overnight.

HE04.note: SoC=0. NonSpin 20 MW. YE load bottom-2 HE includes HE04-05. Not charging: $23.49 is $4.42/MWh above solar trough minimum. Wait for HE09-10.

HE05.note: SoC=0. NonSpin 20 MW. YE load nadir HE05 = 47,942 MW. Not charging: $27.44 is $8.37 above HE09. P(DA>RT) and P(DA<RT) are near-equal — no strong signal.

HE06.note: Idle. DA $29.81 — too high to charge vs HE09 opportunity. AS prices negligible (<$1/MWh at HE06 per Smartbidder). No offering.

HE07.note: ECRS 40 MW DA. $0.99 x 40 = $40. Structural ECRS window HE07-09 (confirmed multiple prior days). SoC=0 at start; ECRS absorb direction requires SoC headroom — confirm eligibility at SoC=0 with Tenaska (absorb = charge direction, may be eligible).

HE08.note: ECRS 40 MW DA. $1.09 x 40 = $43. DA $19.97 (HE08 = second cheapest but not charging here — preserving full 100 MW capacity for clean HE09-10 charge sequence). Solar begins ramp. Outage peak HE08-09 ~17,693 MW.

HE09.note: CHARGE HOUR 1/3. DA $19.07 — cheapest hour of day (Smartbidder bottom-2). 100 MW x 0.85 RTE = 85 MWh SoC. Net load trough approaching (HE10 = 23,827 MW). Solar 27,423 MW peak at HE13 approaching. DA charge at $19.07 vs RT $18.00 DA-RT spread +$1.07 — small positive, locking in DA certainty vs RT oversupply risk. Full 100 MW to maximize cheap charge window.

HE10.note: CHARGE HOUR 2/3. DA $19.97 — net load trough 23,827 MW (YE minimum). Solar 27,423 MW peak proximity. RT $17.00 est (RT lower than DA — RT oversupply risk more severe). Locking DA at $19.97 is prudent. 100 MW x 0.85 = 85 MWh SoC.

HE11.note: TOP-OFF CHARGE to reach 200 MWh SoC. Need 30 MWh SoC = 35 MWh grid draw (30/0.85=35.3). DA $20.62. Solar peak plateau HE11-15. Net load trough still 25,546 MW (Enverus 27,163 MW HE10). Completes charge to full capacity for maximum HE20-21 discharge revenue.

HE12.note: SoC=200 MWh. NonSpin 20 MW ($1.62 x 20 = $32) + ECRS 20 MW ($0.96 x 20 = $19) = $51. Solar 27,423 MW (peak). WEST_TO_NORTH congestion low (8-12%). Energy not dischargeable — DA $24.60 is far below HE20 calibrated $48.01 opportunity cost.

HE13.note: Solar peak HE13 = 27,423 MW. NonSpin $0.65 x 20 = $13 + ECRS $0.38 x 20 = $8 = $21. AS prices lowest mid-day. SoC preservation. DA $28.00 vs HE20 $48.01 — holding SoC.

HE14.note: GR_WEST wind trough (6,195 MW — elevated vs prior days). WEST_TO_NORTH congestion LOW per congestion-analyst. NonSpin $0.59 x 20 + ECRS $0.37 x 20 = $19. SoC hold.

HE15.note: Solar plateau HE11-15 per both Yes Energy and Enverus. NonSpin + ECRS = $20. SoC=200 MWh locked in.

HE16.note: DA rising ($34.96). Solar still 23,212-25,000 MW range. NonSpin $0.85 x 20 + ECRS $0.38 x 20 = $25. Energy at $34.96 vs HE20 $48.01 — still holding SoC. Discharging here would give up $13/MWh of value × 100 MWh = $1,300 opportunity cost.

HE17.note: Load 72,000+ MW (near-peak). DA $32.03 (slightly lower than HE16 — pre-peak shoulder). Solar declining. NonSpin $1.01 x 20 + ECRS $0.46 x 20 = $29. SoC preservation.

HE18.note: Load peak HE18 = 76,867 MW (Yes Energy). Solar 23,212 MW — still strong, net load 36,295 MW. DA $42.04 is tempting but: (a) HE20 calibrated $48.01 is higher, (b) discharging 100 MWh at HE18 loses 100 MWh at HE20 → net -$600 opportunity cost. NonSpin $1.23 x 20 + ECRS $1.33 x 20 = $51. SoC hold absolute.

HE19.da_price_source: Smartbidder row 18 (HE19 = 18:00-19:00 CT, i.e. HE19 ERCOT)
HE19.note: CRITICAL AS HOUR. Smartbidder CSV row 18 timestamp = 2026-05-29T19:00 CDT. In ERCOT convention HE19 = hour ending 19:00 CT (18:00-19:00 CT). The Smartbidder DA Ancillary row 228 block = HE19 AS prices: NonSpin=$20.67, RegUp=$25.54, RRS=$12.10, ECRS=$3.75. Deploying AS-heavy: NonSpin 30 MW ($20.67 x 30 = $620) + ECRS 30 MW ($3.75 x 30 = $113) + RRS 20 MW ($12.10 x 20 = $242) = $975 DA gross. Energy DA $60.01 at this hour would yield $6,001 but this IS HE19 not HE20 — and HE20 ($60.01) + HE21 ($53.42) dual discharge yields $9,075 combined. Holding full SoC. Structural ECRS/NonSpin HE19 spike confirmed from 2026-05-24/25/26 learnings.

HE20.note: PRIMARY DISCHARGE HOUR 1/2. Smartbidder row 19 timestamp 2026-05-29T20:00 CDT = HE20 ERCOT. DA $60.01 calibrated to $48.01 (x0.80 per 2026-05-24 learning). P(DA>RT)=74.8%. DA-RT spread +$19.82 — strongest short-DA signal of the day. Net load 49,815 MW. Solar near-zero by HE20. Thermal cap-out ~15,525 MW tightens reserves. 100 MW full discharge. Revenue = $48.01 x 100 = $4,801. AS capacity fully committed to energy — not splitting.

HE21.note: PRIMARY DISCHARGE HOUR 2/2. Smartbidder row 20 = $53.42 DA. Calibrated $42.74 (x0.80). P(DA>RT)=72.9%. Spread +$14.73. Net load 51,783 MW (YE peak — highest of day). Solar = 0. Wind beginning recovery (~13,500-16,290 MW per YE by HE21) partially dampens RT. 100 MW full discharge. Revenue = $42.74 x 100 = $4,274.

HE22.note: KEY AS HOUR. SoC=0 post-discharge. NonSpin 50 MW DA at $27.39/MWh = $1,370 gross. RT drag est ~-$274 (20%) net ~$1,096. This is the highest-value AS slot of the day. NonSpin at SoC=0 is eligible (no SoC required for dispatch — confirm with Tenaska). RRS/ECRS not offered: require SoC headroom for up-regulation. DA $44.96 — cannot discharge (SoC=0). P(DA>RT)=73.1% continues but no energy available.

HE23.note: SoC=0. NonSpin 40 MW DA at $20.15/MWh = $806 gross. Wind recovering (YE HE23 = ~18,000 MW). RT drag est ~-$161 net ~$645. Reduced MW vs HE22 (40 vs 50) reflecting wind recovery dampening further price spikes. DA $39.96 elevated but no SoC for energy.

HE24.note: SoC=0. Wind peak (YE HE24 = 21,319 MW). DA $26.61 — overnight recovery. NonSpin 20 MW at $2.60/MWh = $52. Low value; minimal offering. End-of-day SoC=0.

soc_trace.rte_check: 235 MWh grid draw x 0.85 = 199.75 MWh ~ 200 MWh SoC. Valid.

alternative_stack_b.trigger: If DAM clearing (results ~13:00 CT) shows HE20 calibrated DA < $40.00 OR HE21 calibrated DA < $35.00
alternative_stack_b.action: Cancel HE20-21 energy discharge bids. Pivot to AS-heavy: NonSpin 50 MW + ECRS 30 MW HE19-22. Expected revenue under Stack B: ~$3,500-$5,500. Stack B is a significant downgrade — only execute if energy prices definitively fail at DAM.

before_1000_ct: 1. Confirm starting SoC via Tenaska HSL/PMin. If SoC > 0: adjust HE09-11 charge volume per Risk 3 contingency (full schedule at SoC=0; reduce proportionally if SoC > 0 to avoid exceeding 200 MWh).
2. Submit DA Energy charge bids: HE09 -100 MW at max $19.07, HE10 -100 MW at max $19.97, HE11 -35 MW at max $20.62.
3. Submit DA Energy discharge bids: HE20 +100 MW at min $48.01 (Smartbidder $60.01 x0.80), HE21 +100 MW at min $42.74 (Smartbidder $53.42 x0.80).

4. Submit DA AS bids: HE01-02 NonSpin 30 MW each; HE03-05 NonSpin 20 MW each; HE07-08 ECRS 40 MW each; HE12-18 ECRS 20 MW + NonSpin 20 MW each; HE19 NonSpin 30 MW + ECRS 30 MW + RRS 20 MW; HE22 NonSpin 50 MW; HE23 NonSpin 40 MW; HE24 NonSpin 20 MW.
5. RegUp NOT submitted any hour. HE20 DA RegUp $34.00 is attractive but all capacity committed to energy at $48.01.
6. Check ERCOT capacity monitor for forced outage additions by 09:30 CT. Any addition > 1,000 MW HE19-21 warrants re-evaluation of RT retention.
7. Confirm NonSpin SoC=0 eligibility at HE22-24 with Tenaska (ongoing open item).
8. Post-DAM (~13:00 CT): Verify HE20-21 clearing prices. If below calibrated thresholds, evaluate Stack B pivot.
